interpretation: Store visits, exchange attempts, and in-person return friction reflect low service quality in physical retail touchpoints.
Rude staff, poor support, and failed complaint handling indicate frontline customer-service breakdowns.
Wrong items, sizing problems, and poor product quality reflect fulfilment and outcome-quality failures.
Refund disputes, repeated contact attempts, and unresolved service recovery reflect accumulated negative past experience.
Order delays, shipping disruption, and cancellation failures capture logistics-related low service quality.
Non-delivery, delayed arrival, and parcel problems indicate severe fulfilment failure and unmet service promises.
Gift-card and store-credit disputes indicate payment and restitution service failures.
Poor product quality and cheapness reflect core product-related service failure.
Shipping and fulfilment delays indicate operational low service quality in online ordering.
